checklist.question: Did receivables grow materially faster than revenue across consecutive periods?

evidence.quote: AccountsReceivableNetCurrent=125383500.94 (2014-09-30) growing to 145877910.61 (2015-09-30), a 16.3% increase, while SalesRevenueServicesNet=130897530.27 (Q3 2014) grew to 135923826.29 (Q3 2015), only 3.8%

checklist.question: Did non-current 'soft' assets (intangibles, other assets, capitalized items) grow materially faster than the business overall?

evidence.quote: Goodwill=42372732.58 (2010-12-31) growing to Goodwill=157006879.61 (2014-12-31), ~3.7x, roughly in line with SalesRevenueServicesNet=164517492.11 (FY2010) growing to 510500608.32 (FY2014), ~3.1x
evidence.location: 10-K FY2014, Balance Sheet / Income Statement comparatives

checklist.question: Is there a persistent divergence between net income and operating cash flow?

evidence.quote: NetIncomeLoss=7299181.31 (FY2012) vs NetCashProvidedByUsedInOperatingActivitiesContinuingOperations=40095042.04 (FY2012); NetIncomeLoss=11921835.97 (FY2013) vs CFO=51447455.36 (FY2013); NetIncomeLoss=7054113.34 (FY2014) vs CFO=46961750.46 (FY2014)
evidence.location: 10-K FY2014, Cash Flow Statement / Income Statement

checklist.question: Do inventory levels or gross margins show trends inconsistent with revenue?

evidence.quote: No Inventory-related XBRL concept is present anywhere in the provided dataset; GrossProfit=68188961.41 vs SalesRevenueServicesNet=135923826.29 (Q3 2015) implies gross margin ~50.2%, down from ~55% in prior quarters (e.g., GrossProfit=71103348.16 / SalesRevenueServicesNet=128466071.59, Q1 2015 = 55.3%)

checklist.question: Is the earnings trajectory unusually smooth relative to the volatility of revenue and cash flow?

evidence.quote: NetIncomeLoss=605462.04 (Q1 2015), NetIncomeLoss=-6093062.48 (Q2 2015), NetIncomeLoss=-10816627.47 (Q3 2015) show sharp swings into large losses, inconsistent with smoothed earnings

checklist.question: Did liability, allowance, or reserve balances decline (or fail to grow) despite growing business activity?

evidence.quote: AllowanceForDoubtfulAccountsReceivableCurrent=588643.65 (2010-12-31), ~1.71% of AccountsReceivableNetCurrent=34345555.24 (2010-12-31); AllowanceForDoubtfulAccountsReceivableCurrent=245067.97 (2011-12-31), only ~0.40% of AccountsReceivableNetCurrent=60812896.03 (2011-12-31); allowance ratio remained low at 0.65% by 2013 (679943.49 / 103968885.07, 2013-12-31) even as receivables nearly tripled
evidence.location: 10-K FY2012 / 10-K FY2014 Balance Sheets, Allowance footnote

checklist.question: Does the filing chronology show irregularities (late filings/NT forms, amendments, unusual 8-K frequency)?

evidence.quote: 10-K filed 2012-03-29 followed shortly by a 10-K/A filed 2012-04-26; multiple 8-K/A amendments filed on 2012-03-05, 2012-10-24, and 2015-08-14
evidence.location: Filing chronology list, forms 10-K/A and 8-K/A entries

checklist.question: Is the provided data sufficient in coverage and length for the above assessments?

evidence.quote: Quarterly and annual data spanning FY2009 through Q3 2015 provided across Revenue, AR, Allowance, Goodwill, Intangibles, CFO, NetIncome, Liabilities, and other concepts, though Inventory and detailed tax/reserve line items are absent

mechanism_hypotheses.accounting_treatment: Potential premature or aggressive revenue recognition (e.g., extended customer payment terms, bill-and-hold, or channel stuffing) causing receivables to build up faster than sales, evidenced by DSO expansion; combined with an allowance for doubtful accounts that was not scaled up proportionally to the receivables base, understating the bad-debt reserve and overstating net receivables and net income.

rationale_evidence.quote: AccountsReceivableNetCurrent grew 31.6% (103968885.07 to 136824811.47, FY2013 to FY2014) while SalesRevenueServicesNet grew only 12.5% (453889907.19 to 510500608.32) over the same period
AccountsReceivableNetCurrent grew 16.3% year-over-year to 145877910.61 (2015-09-30) while SalesRevenueServicesNet grew only 3.8% to 135923826.29 (Q3 2015)
AllowanceForDoubtfulAccountsReceivableCurrent fell from 1.71% of AR (588643.65/34345555.24, 2010-12-31) to just 0.40% of AR (245067.97/60812896.03, 2011-12-31) even as AR nearly doubled

mechanism_hypotheses.accounting_treatment: Cash flow from operations consistently and increasingly exceeds net income by a wide, growing margin not fully explained by depreciation and amortization add-backs, suggesting reliance on favorable working-capital timing (e.g., stretching payables, front-loading deferred revenue collections) to support reported cash generation while earnings quality/comparability with cash results weakens.

rationale_evidence.quote: NetIncomeLoss=7299181.31 (FY2012) vs CFO=40095042.04 (FY2012), a gap of ~32.8M versus DepreciationAndAmortization=20821166.96 (FY2012)
NetIncomeLoss=11921835.97 (FY2013) vs CFO=51447455.36 (FY2013), a gap of ~39.5M versus DepreciationAndAmortization=25112259.07 (FY2013)
NetIncomeLoss=7054113.34 (FY2014) vs CFO=46961750.46 (FY2014), a gap of ~39.9M versus DepreciationAndAmortization=23495290.99 (FY2014)

mechanism_hypotheses.accounting_treatment: Deteriorating gross margin and a sudden swing to large operating and net losses in Q2-Q3 2015 (following a period of receivables/DSO build-up) is consistent with a delayed recognition of previously overstated revenue quality or write-offs/credits catching up to prior aggressive recognition, which would understate prior-period expense/reserves and overstate prior-period earnings.

rationale_evidence.quote: OperatingIncomeLoss=-4749993.89 (Q2 2015) and OperatingIncomeLoss=-8611015.74 (Q3 2015), a sharp reversal from OperatingIncomeLoss=2253664.28 (Q1 2015) and prior consistent profitability

top_signals: Accounts receivable growing materially faster than revenue in 2014 and through Q3 2015 (DSO expanding from ~79 to ~98 days)
Allowance for doubtful accounts sharply underfunded relative to receivables growth in 2011-2013 (ratio fell from 1.71% to as low as 0.40% of AR)
Persistent and widening gap between net income and operating cash flow (CFO 4-7x reported net income in 2012-2014) not fully explained by D&A
Sudden reversal into large net losses and margin compression in Q2-Q3 2015 following the receivables build-up, and presence of 10-K/A and multiple 8-K/A filing amendments
